pattern: Every element is either in or out of a subset, so enumerating that binary choice for each element produces all distinct subsets exactly once.
hints: The collection of all subsets is built by deciding, independently for each element, whether to include it, which is a clean two-way branch.
A recursive walk over the elements that records the current selection at each leaf, or that appends each element to copies of the subsets found so far, both enumerate them all.
Backtrack from a start index: record the current partial subset, then for each later element add it, recurse, and remove it, so no subset is produced twice.

edge_inputs.description: unsorted input still yields all subsets

stress.description: small unique-valued arrays so the 2^n enumeration stays cheap
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, 8)
    vals = rng.sample(range(-10, 11), n)
    return [vals]
stress.note: sample guarantees uniqueness as the constraints require; n capped at 8
